name: ExtensionIngredient_AllergenicIndicator
title: R5: If the ingredient is a known or suspected allergen (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` 0..1 `boolean`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` 0..1 `boolean`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Ingredient` is not mapped.
Element `Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: If the ingredient is a known or suspected allergen (new)
element.comment:
Element `Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `Ingredient` is not mapped.
Element `Ingredient.allergenicIndicator` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: If the ingredient is a known or suspected allergen
element.definition: If the ingredient is a known or suspected allergen. Note that this is a property of the substance, so if a reference to a SubstanceDefinition is used to decribe that (rather than just a code), the allergen information should go there, not here.
